response:
This is the standard bottom-up dynamic programming implementation of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) algorithm, which computes the *length* (not the actual subsequence itself) of the longest sequence of elements present in both input sequences `a` and `b` while preserving relative order (elements do not need to be contiguous).
It fills a 2D DP lookup table by incrementing the LCS count when matching elements are found, otherwise carrying forward the best valid LCS length from previously solved smaller subproblems.
